title: Families/Friends of Individuals With an Alcohol Use Disorder Support Groups - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Families/Friends of Individuals With an Alcohol Use Disorder Support Groups 2 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF AL-ANON FAMILY GROUPS AL-ANON AND ALATEEN SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a mutual support program for people whose lives have been affected by someone else’s drinking, whether or not the alcoholic admits the existence of a drinking problem or seeks help. Alateen is a fellowship of young people (mostly teenagers) whose lives have been affected by someone else’s drinking. Meetings are available in-person, by phone, and online. 1750 SW Skyline Boulevard Suite 133 Portland, OR 97221 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies by location Email (888) 425-2666 - Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILIES ANONYMOUS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A 12-step support program for families and friends of individuals with drug, alcohol, or related behavioral issues. Support group meetings are available both in person and online depending on the area. Audio and video recordings available on agency website. Locator Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies by service Email (800) 736-9805 Visit Website More Details AL-ANON FAMILY GROUPS AL-ANON AND ALATEEN SUPPORT GROUPS
